NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the sake of the faith of God’s elect and the knowledge of the truth that is in accordance with godliness,
text.Rotherham: Paul, a servant of God—an apostle moreover of Jesus Christ,—according to the faith of the chosen ones of God, and the personal knowledge of the truth that is according to godliness,—
text.KJV: Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;
text.NABRE: Paul, a slave of God and apostle of Jesus Christ for the sake of the faith of God’s chosen ones and the recognition of religious truth,
text.DRB: Paul the servant of God, and an Apostle of Jesus Christ according to the faith of the elect of God and knowledge of the truth: which is according to piety,

text.NRSV: in the hope of eternal life that God, who never lies, promised before the ages began—
text.Rotherham: In hope of life age-abiding; which God, who cannot lie, promised before age-during times,
text.KJV: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;
text.NABRE: in the hope of eternal life that God, who does not lie, promised before time began,
text.DRB: into the hope of life everlasting, which he promised that lieth not, God, before the secular times:

text.NRSV: in due time he revealed his word through the proclamation with which I have been entrusted by the command of God our Savior,
text.Rotherham: but hath manifested in its fitting seasons, even his word, in the proclamation with which entrusted am I—by injunction of our Saviour God:
text.KJV: But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
text.NABRE: who indeed at the proper time revealed his word in the proclamation with which I was entrusted by the command of God our savior,
text.DRB: but hath manifested in due times his word in preaching, which is committed to me according to the precept of our Saviour God:

text.NRSV: To Titus, my loyal child in the faith we share: Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
text.Rotherham: Unto Titus, my true child according to a common faith,—favour and peace, from God [our] Father and Christ Jesus our Saviour.
text.KJV: To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.
text.NABRE: to Titus, my true child in our common faith: grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.
text.DRB: to Titus my beloved son according to the common faith, grace and peace from God the Father, and Christ Jesus our Saviour.

text.NRSV: I left you behind in Crete for this reason, so that you should put in order what remained to be done, and should appoint elders in every town, as I directed you:
text.Rotherham: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that, the things remaining undone, thou mightest completely set in order, and mightest establish, in every city, elders, as, I, with thee arranged:—
text.KJV: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:
text.NABRE: For this reason I left you in Crete so that you might set right what remains to be done and appoint presbyters in every town, as I directed you,
text.DRB: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest reform the things that are wanting, and shouldest ordain priests by cities, as I also appointed thee:

text.NRSV: someone who is blameless, married only once, whose children are believers, not accused of debauchery and not rebellious.
text.Rotherham: If anyone is unaccusable, a husband of, one wife, having children that believe, who are not charged with riotous excess, nor insubordinate;
text.KJV: If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.
text.NABRE: on condition that a man be blameless, married only once, with believing children who are not accused of licentiousness or rebellious.
text.DRB: if any be without crime, the husband of one wife, having faithful children, not in the accusation of riot, or not obedient.

text.NRSV: For a bishop, as God’s steward, must be blameless; he must not be arrogant or quick-tempered or addicted to wine or violent or greedy for gain;
text.Rotherham: For it is needful that the overseer be—unaccusable, as God’s steward, not self-willed, not soon angry, not given to wine, not ready to wound, not seeking gain by base means,
text.KJV: For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;
text.NABRE: For a bishop as God’s steward must be blameless, not arrogant, not irritable, not a drunkard, not aggressive, not greedy for sordid gain,
text.DRB: For a Bishop must be without crime, as the steward of God: not proud, not angry, not given to wine, no striker, not covetous of filthy lucre:

text.NRSV: but he must be hospitable, a lover of goodness, prudent, upright, devout, and self-controlled.
text.Rotherham: But hospitable, a lover of what is good, sober-minded, just, kind, possessing self-control,
text.KJV: But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;
text.NABRE: but hospitable, a lover of goodness, temperate, just, holy, and self-controlled,
text.DRB: but given to hospitality, gentle, sober, just, holy, continent:

text.NRSV: He must have a firm grasp of the word that is trustworthy in accordance with the teaching, so that he may be able both to preach with sound doctrine and to refute those who contradict it.
text.Rotherham: Holding fast, in the matter of his teaching, the faithful word, that he may be able both to encourage with his healthful instruction, and, the gainsayers, to refute.
text.KJV: Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.
text.NABRE: holding fast to the true message as taught so that he will be able both to exhort with sound doctrine and to refute opponents.
text.DRB: embracing that faithful word which is according to doctrine, that he may be able to exhort in sound doctrine, and to reprove them that gainsay it.

text.NRSV: There are also many rebellious people, idle talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision;
text.Rotherham: For there are many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially they of the circumcision,—
text.KJV: For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:
text.NABRE: For there are also many rebels, idle talkers and deceivers, especially the Jewish Christians.
text.DRB: For there be many disobedient, vain-speakers, and seducers, especially they that are of the Circumcision.

text.NRSV: they must be silenced, since they are upsetting whole families by teaching for sordid gain what it is not right to teach.
text.Rotherham: Whose mouths must needs be stopped, men who are upsetting whole houses, teaching the things which ought not [to be taught] —for the sake of base gain.
text.KJV: Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre’s sake.
text.NABRE: It is imperative to silence them, as they are upsetting whole families by teaching for sordid gain what they should not.
text.DRB: Who must be controlled. Who subvert whole houses, teaching the things they ought not, for filthy lucre.

text.NRSV: It was one of them, their very own prophet, who said, “Cretans are always liars, vicious brutes, lazy gluttons.”
text.Rotherham: Said one from among them, a prophet, of their own!—Cretans! always false, mischievous wild-beasts, idle gluttons:
text.KJV: One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.
text.NABRE: One of them, a prophet of their own, once said, “Cretans have always been liars, vicious beasts, and lazy gluttons.”
text.DRB: One of them said, their own proper prophet, The Cretensians always liars, naughty beasts, slothful bellies.

text.NRSV: That testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply, so that they may become sound in the faith,
text.Rotherham: This witness, is true,—for which cause, be reproving them sharply, that they may be healthy in their faith,
text.KJV: This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
text.NABRE: That testimony is true. Therefore, admonish them sharply, so that they may be sound in the faith,
text.DRB: This testimony is true. For the which cause rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith,

text.NRSV: not paying attention to Jewish myths or to commandments of those who reject the truth.
text.Rotherham: Not giving heed to Judaical stories and commandments of men who are turning away from the truth:
text.KJV: Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.
text.NABRE: instead of paying attention to Jewish myths and regulations of people who have repudiated the truth.
text.DRB: not attending to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, averting themselves from the truth.

text.NRSV: To the pure all things are pure, but to the corrupt and unbelieving nothing is pure. Their very minds and consciences are corrupted.
text.Rotherham: All things, are pure, unto the pure, but, unto the polluted and faithless, nothing, is pure, but polluted are both their mind and conscience;
text.KJV: Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.
text.NABRE: To the clean all things are clean, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is clean; in fact, both their minds and their consciences are tainted.
text.DRB: All things are clean to the clean: but to the polluted and to infidels nothing is clean: but polluted are both their mind and conscience.

text.NRSV: They profess to know God, but they deny him by their actions. They are detestable, disobedient, unfit for any good work.
text.Rotherham: God, they confess that they know, but, by their works, they deny him, being, abominable, and obdurate, and, as to any good work, found, worthless.
text.KJV: They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.
text.NABRE: They claim to know God, but by their deeds they deny him. They are vile and disobedient and unqualified for any good deed.
text.DRB: They confess that they know God: but in their works they deny, whereas they be abominable and incredulous and to every good work reprobate.

text.NRSV: But as for you, teach what is consistent with sound doctrine.
text.Rotherham: But do, thou, speak the things which become the healthful instruction:—
text.KJV: But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:
text.NABRE: As for yourself, you must say what is consistent with sound doctrine, namely,
text.DRB: But do thou speak the things that become sound doctrine.

text.NRSV: Tell the older men to be temperate, serious, prudent, and sound in faith, in love, and in endurance.
text.Rotherham: That, aged men, be, sober, grave, sober-minded, healthy in their faith, love, endurance;
text.KJV: That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
text.NABRE: that older men should be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, love, and endurance.
text.DRB: Old men that they be sober, chaste, wise, sound in the faith, in love, in patience.

text.NRSV: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in behavior, not to be slanderers or slaves to drink; they are to teach what is good,
text.Rotherham: Aged women, in the same way, in deportment, as becometh sacred persons, not given to intrigue, nor yet, to much wine, enslaved, teachers of virtue,—
text.KJV: The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
text.NABRE: Similarly, older women should be reverent in their behavior, not slanderers, not addicted to drink, teaching what is good,
text.DRB: Old women in like manner, in holy attire, not ill speakers, not given to much wine: teaching well,

text.NRSV: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
text.Rotherham: That they may constrain the young women to be, lovers of their husbands, lovers of their children,
text.KJV: That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,
text.NABRE: so that they may train younger women to love their husbands and children,
text.DRB: that they may teach the young women wisdom, to love their husbands, to love their children,

text.NRSV: to be self-controlled, chaste, good managers of the household, kind, being submissive to their husbands, so that the word of God may not be discredited.
text.Rotherham: soberminded, chaste, workers at home, good, submitting themselves to their own husbands,—that, the word of God, be not defamed;
text.KJV: To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
text.NABRE: to be self-controlled, chaste, good homemakers, under the control of their husbands, so that the word of God may not be discredited.
text.DRB: wise, chaste, sober, having a care of the house, gentle, subject to their husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.

text.NRSV: Likewise, urge the younger men to be self-controlled.
text.Rotherham: The younger men, in the same way, exhort thou to be sober-minded:
text.KJV: Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
text.NABRE: Urge the younger men, similarly, to control themselves,
text.DRB: Young men in like manner exhort that they be sober.

text.NRSV: Show yourself in all respects a model of good works, and in your teaching show integrity, gravity,
text.Rotherham: In all things, shewing, thyself, an ensample of noble works,—in thine instruction, uncorruptness, gravity,
text.KJV: In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,
text.NABRE: showing yourself as a model of good deeds in every respect, with integrity in your teaching, dignity,
text.DRB: In all things shew thyself an example of good works, in doctrine, in integrity, in gravity,

text.NRSV: and sound speech that cannot be censured; then any opponent will be put to shame, having nothing evil to say of us.
text.Rotherham: healthful discourse that cannot be condemned, in order that, he that is of the contrary part, may relent, having nothing to say concerning us that is disparaging:
text.KJV: Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.
text.NABRE: and sound speech that cannot be criticized, so that the opponent will be put to shame without anything bad to say about us.
text.DRB: the word sound, irreprehensible: that he which is on the contrary part, may be afraid, having no evil to say of us.

text.NRSV: Tell slaves to be submissive to their masters and to give satisfaction in every respect; they are not to talk back,
text.Rotherham: Servants, unto their own masters, to be submitting themselves in all things, to be, well-pleasing, not gainsaying,
text.KJV: Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;
text.NABRE: Slaves are to be under the control of their masters in all respects, giving them satisfaction, not talking back to them
text.DRB: Servants to be subject to their masters, in all things pleasing, not gainsaying:

text.NRSV: not to pilfer, but to show complete and perfect fidelity, so that in everything they may be an ornament to the doctrine of God our Savior.
text.Rotherham: not taking anything away, shewing, all good faith,—that, the instruction which is of our Saviour God, they may adorn in all things.
text.KJV: Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
text.NABRE: or stealing from them, but exhibiting complete good faith, so as to adorn the doctrine of God our savior in every way.
text.DRB: not defrauding, but in all things shewing good faith, that they may adorn the doctrine of our Saviour God in all things.

text.NRSV: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all,
text.Rotherham: For the favour of God, bringing salvation for all men, hath shone forth,
text.KJV: For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,
text.NABRE: Transformation of Life. For the grace of God has appeared, saving all
text.DRB: For the grace of God our Saviour hath appeared to all men:

text.NRSV: training us to renounce impiety and worldly passions, and in the present age to live lives that are self-controlled, upright, and godly,
text.Rotherham: Putting us under discipline—in order that,—denying ourselves of ungodliness and worldly covetings, in a soberminded and righteous and godly manner, we should live, in the present age,
text.KJV: Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;
text.NABRE: and training us to reject godless ways and worldly desires and to live temperately, justly, and devoutly in this age,
text.DRB: instructing us that denying impiety and worldly desires, we live soberly, and justly, and godly in this world,

text.NRSV: while we wait for the blessed hope and the manifestation of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Prepared to welcome the happy hope and forthshining of the glory of the great God and our Saviour Christ Jesus,—
text.KJV: Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;
text.NABRE: as we await the blessed hope, the appearance of the glory of the great God and of our savior Jesus Christ,
text.DRB: expecting the blessed hope and advent of the glory of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: He it is who gave himself for us that he might redeem us from all iniquity and purify for himself a people of his own who are zealous for good deeds.
text.Rotherham: Who gave himself up in our behalf, that he might redeem us from all manner of lawlessness, and purify for himself a people as his own treasure—zealous of noble works.
text.KJV: Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.
text.NABRE: who gave himself for us to deliver us from all lawlessness and to cleanse for himself a people as his own, eager to do what is good.
text.DRB: who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and might cleanse to himself a people acceptable, a pursuer of good works.

text.NRSV: Declare these things; exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one look down on you.
text.Rotherham: As to these things, be speaking and exhorting and reproving, with all manner of precept: let, no one, disregard thee!
text.KJV: These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.
text.NABRE: Say these things. Exhort and correct with all authority. Let no one look down on you.
text.DRB: These things speak, and exhort and rebuke with all authority. Let no man contemn thee.

text.NRSV: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
text.Rotherham: Be putting them in mind, unto rulerships, unto authorities, to be in submission, to be yielding to rule, for every good work, to be, prepared,
text.KJV: Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,
text.NABRE: Remind them to be under the control of magistrates and authorities, to be obedient, to be open to every good enterprise.
text.DRB: Admonish them to be subject to Princes and Potestates, to obey at a word, to be ready to every good work,

text.NRSV: to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show every courtesy to everyone.
text.Rotherham: To be defaming, no one, to be, averse to strife, considerate, shewing, all, meekness unto all men.
text.KJV: To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.
text.NABRE: They are to slander no one, to be peaceable, considerate, exercising all graciousness toward everyone.
text.DRB: to blaspheme no man, not to be litigious, but modest: shewing all mildness toward all men.

text.NRSV: For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, led astray, slaves to various passions and pleasures, passing our days in malice and envy, despicable, hating one another.
text.Rotherham: For, even we, used, at one time, to be—thoughtless, unyielding, deceived, in servitude unto manifold covetings and pleasures, in malice and envy, leading on, detestable, hating one another.
text.KJV: For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
text.NABRE: For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, deluded, slaves to various desires and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful ourselves and hating one another.
text.DRB: For we also were sometime unwise, incredulous, erring, serving diverse desires and voluptuousnesses, living in malice and envy, odible, hating one another.

text.NRSV: But when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared,
text.Rotherham: But, when, the graciousness and affection for man of our Saviour God, shone forth,
text.KJV: But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,
text.NABRE: But when the kindness and generous love of God our savior appeared,
text.DRB: But when the benignity and kindness toward man of our Saviour God appeared:

text.NRSV: he saved us, not because of any works of righteousness that we had done, but according to his mercy, through the water of rebirth and renewal by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Not by works which we had done in righteousness, but, according to his mercy, he saved us—through means of the bathing of a new birth, and the moulding anew of Holy Spirit.—
text.KJV: Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;
text.NABRE: not because of any righteous deeds we had done but because of his mercy, he saved us through the bath of rebirth and renewal by the holy Spirit,
text.DRB: not by the works of Justice which we did, but according to his mercy he hath saved us by the laver of regeneration and renovation of the Holy Ghost,

text.NRSV: This Spirit he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
text.Rotherham: Which he poured on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Saviour,
text.KJV: Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
text.NABRE: whom he richly poured out on us through Jesus Christ our savior,
text.DRB: whom he hath poured upon us aboundantly by Jesus Christ our Saviour:

text.NRSV: so that, having been justified by his grace, we might become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
text.Rotherham: In order that, having been declared righteous by his favour, we should be made inheritors, according to hope, of life age-abiding.
text.KJV: That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
text.NABRE: so that we might be justified by his grace and become heirs in hope of eternal life.
text.DRB: that being justified by his grace, we may be heirs according to hope of life everlasting.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure. I desire that you insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works; these things are excellent and profitable to everyone.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! And, concerning these things, I am minded that thou be strongly affirming, in order that they who have believed God may be careful to be forward, in noble works. These things, are honourable, and profitable unto men;
text.KJV: This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good works. These things are good and profitable unto men.
text.NABRE: This saying is trustworthy. Advice to Titus. I want you to insist on these points, that those who have believed in God be careful to devote themselves to good works; these are excellent and beneficial to others.
text.DRB: It is a faithful saying, and of these things I will have thee avouch earnestly: that they which believe in God, be careful to excel in good works. These things be good and profitable for men.

text.NRSV: But avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, dissensions, and quarrels about the law, for they are unprofitable and worthless.
text.Rotherham: But, foolish questionings, and genealogies, and strife, and contentions about matters of law, avoid, for they are unprofitable and vain.
text.KJV: But avoid foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
text.NABRE: Avoid foolish arguments, genealogies, rivalries, and quarrels about the law, for they are useless and futile.
text.DRB: But foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and controversies of the Law avoid. For they are unprofitable and vain.

text.NRSV: After a first and second admonition, have nothing more to do with anyone who causes divisions,
text.Rotherham: From a party-man, after a first and second admonition, excuse thyself;
text.KJV: A man that is an heretick after the first and second admonition reject;
text.NABRE: After a first and second warning, break off contact with a heretic,
text.DRB: Aman that is an heretic after the first and second admonition avoid:

text.NRSV: since you know that such a person is perverted and sinful, being self-condemned.
text.Rotherham: Knowing that such a one is perverted, and sinneth, being self-condemned.
text.KJV: Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
text.NABRE: realizing that such a person is perverted and sinful and stands self-condemned. Directives, Greetings, and Blessing.
text.DRB: knowing that he that is such an one, is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned by his own judgement.

text.NRSV: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
text.Rotherham: As soon as I shall send Artemus unto thee, or Tychicus, give diligence to come to me unto Nicopolis; for, there, have I determined to winter.
text.KJV: When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
text.NABRE: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, try to join me at Nicopolis, where I have decided to spend the winter.
text.DRB: When I shall send to thee Artemas or Tychicus, hasten to come unto me to Nicopolis. For there I have determined to winter.

text.NRSV: Make every effort to send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way, and see that they lack nothing.
text.Rotherham: Zenas the lawyer, and Apollos, do thou diligently set forward on their way, in order that, nothing, unto them, may be wanting.
text.KJV: Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.
text.NABRE: Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey soon, and see to it that they have everything they need.
text.DRB: Set forward Zenas the lawyer and Apollos carefully, that nothing be wanting to them.

text.NRSV: And let people learn to devote themselves to good works in order to meet urgent needs, so that they may not be unproductive.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, let our own learn to be forward, in honourable works, for the necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.
text.KJV: And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
text.NABRE: But let our people, too, learn to devote themselves to good works to supply urgent needs, so that they may not be unproductive.
text.DRB: And let our men also learn to excel in good works to necessary uses: that they be not unfruitful.

text.NRSV: All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: All they who are with me salute thee. Salute thou them who regard us with faithful affection. Favour, be with you all.
text.KJV: All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen. It was written to Titus, ordained the first bishop of the church of the Cretians, from Nicopolis of Macedonia.
text.NABRE: All who are with me send you greetings. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you.
text.DRB: All that are with me, salute thee: salute them that love us in the faith. The grace of God be with you all. Amen.
